metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `novelty(sub_answers: dict) -> float` as a named, exported function to `decide.py` (mirroring `validity()`), and confirm both `score_novelty` in `verify.py` and `_novelty()` in `eval_scorers.py` call it — without this, the 'scorer and gate can never diverge' guarantee is structurally unenforceable.
findings.finding: `decide.novelty()` is referenced as a callable function ("the shared `decide.novelty` arithmetic", "via the shared `decide.novelty` arithmetic, so the scorer and the gate can never diverge") but is never named as a symbol to add to `decide.py` in the AC list or body text, and does not exist in the current codebase.
findings.location: Plan section 'The `novelty` contract' and '`discriminates_novelty` eval gate'; AC line '`discriminates_novelty` scorer … dedicated `_novelty(out)` extractor'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to `decide.py`: `def novelty(sub_answers: dict[str, Any]) -> float: scores = [_GRADE[sub_answers[q]] for q in NOVELTY_SUBANSWERS if sub_answers.get(q) in ('yes', 'insufficient', 'no')]; return round(1.0 - sum(scores)/len(scores), 4) if scores else 0.0` and export it from `review_kernel/__init__.py`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that `verify.py` gains a `register_novelty_contract()` function (mirroring `register_verification_contract()` at line 142) called at module load, and that `passes.py`'s `register_contracts()` adds `contracts.register_contract('plan_review_novelty', novelty_model)` — ensuring both the kernel and gate aliases are registered on import.
findings.finding: The module-load registration call for the new `novelty` kernel contract is not specified. The plan states `novelty_model` lives in `verify.py` and is registered under kernel name `novelty`, but the existing registration pattern (`register_verification_contract()` called at module load, line 148 of `verify.py`) is not replicated for the novelty contract — no analogous `register_novelty_contract()` call or equivalent is named in the plan text or AC list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the token-budget chunking strategy for `score_novelty`: state whether it reuses `verify_request_chunks` (or a novelty-specific variant), how `window_tokens`/`est_tokens` are injected, and how findings with no novelty output (due to chunking omissions or timeouts) are handled — already defaulting to 0.0 per the fail-safe, but the chunking seam itself needs to be named.
findings.finding: `score_novelty` is described as 'mirroring `verify_findings`' but the plan is silent on token-budget chunking for the novelty sub-call. `verify_findings` uses `verify_request_chunks` to handle large finding sets; the novelty sub-call additionally injects all prior findings as context, making prompt size potentially larger than the verification call. Without specifying a chunking strategy, a review with many findings against a large prior-finding set could silently fail or time out, with every affected finding then defaulting to novelty=0.0 (carryover), making the novelty signal vacuous for large runs.
findings.location: Plan section: 'The sub-call mechanism + data-flow contract' / AC: `score_novelty` in `verify.py`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC requiring the `plan-review-novelty` prompt to be authored and registered in the prompt library (e.g. 'the `plan-review-novelty` prompt exists in the prompt library and `rebar prompt eval plan-review-novelty` resolves it without error').
findings.finding: The plan describes a `plan-review-novelty` prompt as a first-class deliverable (it is the prompt-library id used by the novelty sub-call and the key to the dedicated eval spec), but no acceptance criterion requires the prompt to exist, be authored, or be registered in the prompt library.
findings.location: The novelty contract section / discriminates_novelty section / AC list (all ACs)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a criterion: 'The `plan-review-novelty` prompt is authored, registered in the prompt library under the id `plan-review-novelty`, and successfully resolved by `rebar prompt eval plan-review-novelty`.' This can be combined with existing AC 9 or added as a new AC between AC 5 and AC 6.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify whether `plan-review-novelty.eval.yaml` will carry a `gold_set` (and what its `input`/`label` shape is for a novelty sub-call output), or whether `validate_dataset_and_gold` will be updated to exempt novelty-only specs from the gold_set requirement — the current plan omits this, which would cause `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)` to reject the spec under the existing rule at `eval.py:260-261`.
findings.finding: The plan's extension to `validate_dataset_and_gold` adds a novelty-axis balance check but does not address the existing mandatory `gold_set` requirement (eval.py:260-261: 'strict: eval spec needs a non-empty gold_set'). A novelty-only spec with only `high_novelty`/`low_novelty` dataset cases and no `gold_set` would fail `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)` — the CI discipline gate that runs over all packaged specs. The plan describes ≥3 labeled dataset pairs but never mentions a `gold_set` for the novelty spec.
findings.location: AC line: '`ALLOWED_EXPECTS` extended … AND `validate_dataset_and_gold` in `eval.py` … so `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)` both accepts the cases and still enforces a balanced discrimination set'; eval.py:259-265

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC item (and a plan section) specifying the creation of the `plan-review-novelty` prompt file (e.g. `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_novelty.md`), including its contract binding (`outputs: plan_review_novelty`) and the prior-findings context injection pattern.
findings.finding: The `plan-review-novelty` prompt file is a missing edit target: the plan names `prompt: plan-review-novelty` as the prompt-library id the novelty sub-call uses (and the key for the dedicated eval spec), but no AC or section specifies creating the corresponding prompt file. The sibling artifacts — `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_verifier.md` and `plan_review_verifier_agentic.md` — confirm prompt files must exist for a sub-call to be dispatchable, yet no `plan_review_novelty.md` (or equivalent) is named anywhere in the plan.
findings.location: AC line: 'Novelty scored in a separate Pass-2 sub-call … score_novelty'; `discriminates_novelty` section: 'prompt: plan-review-novelty'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: 'A `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_novelty.md` prompt file exists, declares `outputs: plan_review_novelty`, and embeds the three matches-prior questions in a NOVELTY_SUBANSWERS-aligned format.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the unconditional `gold_set` requirement in `validate_dataset_and_gold` for the novelty spec: either specify a `gold_set` for `plan-review-novelty.eval.yaml`, or make the `gold_set` check conditional on the spec containing an LLM-judge scorer (since the requirement's purpose is kappa-alignment, not deterministic scorers).
findings.finding: The plan requires `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)` to accept the dedicated `plan-review-novelty.eval.yaml`, but `validate_dataset_and_gold` in `eval.py` (lines 259-265) unconditionally requires a non-empty `gold_set` under strict mode. The novelty spec has only deterministic scorers — no LLM-judge requiring kappa alignment — so there is no natural `gold_set` to supply. The plan extends `validate_dataset_and_gold` with a novelty-axis balance check but says nothing about this pre-existing unconditional guard; the dedicated spec will fail strict validation without either specifying a `gold_set` for the novelty spec or making the `gold_set` check conditional on the presence of an LLM-judge scorer.
findings.location: AC: `validate_dataset_and_gold` in `eval.py` extended with novelty-axis balance check; `eval.py` lines 259-265
findings.suggested_fix: In `validate_dataset_and_gold`, gate the gold_set check on whether the spec contains at least one `llm-judge` scorer: `if any(s.get('type') == 'llm-judge' for s in spec.get('scorers', [])): # enforce gold_set`. This keeps the kappa-alignment requirement for judge-bearing specs while allowing purely-deterministic specs (like the novelty spec) to pass strict validation without an unnecessary gold_set.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether 'answerable' means the LLM explicitly returned a non-default value (requiring a sentinel or `None` default rather than `"insufficient"`) or whether `insufficient` at default is treated as unanswerable — and align the `novelty_model` field defaults and the `decide.novelty()` implementation to match, so the 'no answerable sub-answers → novelty 0.0' branch is reachable.
findings.finding: The 'answerable' definition is ambiguous with respect to the default value `insufficient`: all three sub-answer fields default to `"insufficient"`, which is a member of `{yes, insufficient, no}` (i.e., 'answerable'). This makes the 'no answerable sub-answers → novelty 0.0' branch unreachable under the default-fill behavior, contradicting the plan's intent that a degenerate/empty LLM response is treated as carryover.
findings.location: Plan section 'Novelty sub-answer set + scoring arithmetic (canonical)'; AC line 'no answerable sub-answers → novelty 0.0'
findings.suggested_fix: Use `None` as the default for the three sub-answer fields in `novelty_model` (not `"insufficient"`) so a missing field is skipped in the `decide.novelty()` arithmetic; only an explicitly-returned `insufficient` is graded. Reserve `"insufficient"` as a meaningful LLM answer, not a Pydantic fill value.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add unit tests in `tests/unit/test_plan_review_novelty.py` for the `decide.novelty` arithmetic: (1) happy path with all-yes/all-no/mixed sub-answers producing the correct novelty float, (2) partial-answerability case where blank/missing sub-answers are skipped in the mean, and (3) no-answerable-sub-answers path triggering the 0.0 default via the arithmetic (not the sub-call-error fallback).
findings.finding: The plan's test specification covers the sub-call failure/fallback path (FakeRunner raising/returning junk) but names NO unit test for the happy-path behavior of `score_novelty` or for the `decide.novelty` arithmetic edge cases — specifically: all sub-answers answerable and varied (yes/insufficient/no mix), partial answerability (some sub-answers blank/missing → skipped), and all sub-answers non-answerable → novelty defaults to 0.0. The `novelty = 1 − mean(answerable sub-answers)` formula with the ordinal map (yes=1.0, insufficient=0.5, no=0.0) is newly introduced logic with precise numeric semantics; bugs in it would silently corrupt the carryover signal passed to cc5b.
findings.location: AC: 'Fail-safe fallback … covered by a FakeRunner test in tests/unit/test_plan_review_novelty.py'; `decide.py` NOVELTY_SUBANSWERS / decide.novelty; AC: 'novelty = 1 − mean(answerable matches-prior sub-answers)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `test_decide_novelty_arithmetic` parametrized test to `tests/unit/test_plan_review_novelty.py` with cases: `({restates_prior_defect: 'yes', cites_prior_location: 'yes', matches_prior_fix: 'yes'}, 0.0)`, `({...: 'no', ...: 'no', ...: 'no'}, 1.0)`, `({...: 'yes', ...: 'insufficient', ...: 'no'}, round(1-(1.0+0.5+0.0)/3, 9))`, `({...: '', ...: '', ...: ''}, 0.0)` (no answerable → default 0.0), and `({...: 'yes', ...: '', ...: 'no'}, 0.5)` (two answerable, one blank skipped).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `review_kernel/__init__.py` as an explicit edit target, enumerating the new `NOVELTY_SUBANSWERS` constant and `novelty()` function from `decide.py` to be imported and added to `__all__`.
findings.finding: `review_kernel/__init__.py` is an unenumerated consumer: the plan places `NOVELTY_SUBANSWERS` and the `decide.novelty(...)` arithmetic function in `decide.py`, but `review_kernel/__init__.py` explicitly re-exports every `decide.py` symbol in its `__all__` list (currently: `DEFAULT_BLOCK_THRESHOLD`, `GRADED_BINARY`, `impact`, `pass3_decide`, `pass3_over_findings`, `severity_label`, `validity`). The plan does not name `review_kernel/__init__.py` as a file to update, so new decide.py symbols would not be surfaced through the kernel's public surface.
findings.location: `Novelty sub-answer set + scoring arithmetic` section; `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/__init__.py:45–53` (the decide imports block)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `novelty_model` should include a `novelty: float` output field (making the extractor's first branch live) or whether `_novelty(out)` always computes from the three sub-answers (making the 'numeric novelty' branch description inaccurate and removable).
findings.finding: The `_novelty(out)` extractor is described as reading 'numeric `novelty`, else computed from the matches-prior sub-answers', but the `novelty_model` contract schema lists no `novelty: float` field — only `index`, the three sub-answers, and `matched_prior_id`. The first ('numeric novelty') branch is either dead code or implies an undocumented schema addition, leaving an executor uncertain whether to add a float field to the contract or simply ignore the branch.
findings.location: Section 'The `novelty` contract' + Section '`discriminates_novelty` eval gate' — `_novelty(out)` extractor description
findings.suggested_fix: If the intent is to always compute from sub-answers (simpler, consistent with the contract), remove the 'numeric `novelty`, else' clause from the extractor description and document `_novelty(out)` as purely computing `decide.novelty` from the sub-answer fields of the structured output.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether `score_novelty` belongs in `verify.py` (the finding-verifier) or in a dedicated `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/novelty.py` module — the plan's own structural-independence argument (novelty is a separate sub-call with a separate contract) favors the latter; re-exported in `passes.py` either way.
findings.finding: score_novelty is proposed to live inside verify.py, whose name, module docstring, and entire design are exclusively 'the finding-VERIFIER (Pass-2)'. Adding novelty scoring — a semantically distinct concern (carryover detection, not finding validation) — to this module is a golden-hammer co-location: verify.py becomes the generic home for any LLM sub-call that happens to run during Pass-2, regardless of purpose.
findings.location: AC line: '`score_novelty` (... `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/verify.py` ...)'; plan section: 'The sub-call mechanism + data-flow contract'
findings.suggested_fix: Place `score_novelty` + `novelty_model` + `NOVELTY_SUBANSWERS` in a new `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/novelty.py`, mirroring how `verify.py` is separate from `decide.py`. Re-export `score_novelty` from `review_kernel/__init__.py` and `passes.py` as the plan already requires.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the characterization of `VERIFIER_RULES[0]` as an 'independence invariant that holds by construction': it is a soft prompt scaffold rule enforced behaviorally by evals (per `verify.py:59-62`), not a runtime code invariant; the actual independence guarantee lives in the structural separation (separate sub-call that alone sees prior findings) that this task creates.
findings.finding: The plan characterizes `VERIFIER_RULES[0]` at `verify.py:64` as an 'independence invariant' that 'holds by construction' — but reading the actual file reveals it is a soft prompt-scaffold TEXT tuple, with code comments explicitly stating it is 'NOT enforced by a prompt-text lint… enforced BEHAVIORALLY by evals'. The independence is enforced by the structural separation (separate sub-call), not by `VERIFIER_RULES[0]` as a code invariant. Calling it an 'invariant' that 'holds by construction' overstates what the existing symbol provides and may mislead implementors about where the guarantee actually lives.
findings.location: Plan section 'Why an LLM sub-call and not the deterministic `sidecar.norm_id` fingerprint' — phrase: 'the `verify.py:64` independence invariant (VERIFIER_RULES[0]) holds by construction'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the phrasing to: 'the structural independence the separate sub-call provides mirrors the intent of VERIFIER_RULES[0] (`verify.py:64`) — the soft prompt rule that the verifier must never see prior findings — now enforced by construction (data-flow isolation) rather than by prompt assertion alone.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `eval.py:210–216`'s import block as an edit target that must add `NOVELTY_EXPECTS` alongside `IMPACT_EXPECTS` when the novelty-axis balance check is added to `validate_dataset_and_gold`.
findings.finding: The `eval.py` import block inside `validate_dataset_and_gold` (lines 210–216) is an unenumerated consumer: it currently imports `ALLOWED_EXPECTS, FIRE_EXPECTS, IMPACT_EXPECTS, NOFIRE_EXPECTS, VALIDITY_EXPECTS` by name. The plan directs adding `NOVELTY_EXPECTS` to `ALLOWED_EXPECTS` in `eval_scorers.py` and extending `validate_dataset_and_gold` with a novelty-axis balance check, but does not enumerate that the import statement at `eval.py:210–216` must also import `NOVELTY_EXPECTS` for the new balance branch to reference it.
findings.location: AC line: '`ALLOWED_EXPECTS` extended with `NOVELTY_EXPECTS = {high_novelty, low_novelty}` AND `validate_dataset_and_gold` in `eval.py` extended with a novelty-axis balance check'; `eval.py:210–216`
findings.suggested_fix: In the AC or plan body, note: 'eval.py:validate_dataset_and_gold imports NOVELTY_EXPECTS from eval_scorers (alongside the existing IMPACT_EXPECTS import) and uses it in the new novelty-axis balance branch.'

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm the `validate_dataset_and_gold` function's inner import in `eval.py` is extended to include `NOVELTY_EXPECTS` alongside the existing imports, so the novelty balance-check branch can reference it without a `NameError`.
findings.finding: The `validate_dataset_and_gold` balance-check extension requires `NOVELTY_EXPECTS` to be imported inside the function, but the existing import block (lines 210-216 of `eval.py`) is a fixed, explicit list. The plan says `ALLOWED_EXPECTS` is extended and the balance check is added, but does not specify updating this inner import to include `NOVELTY_EXPECTS` — an omission that will cause a `NameError` or silent failure at the novelty balance-check branch.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `NOVELTY_EXPECTS,` to the existing `from rebar.llm.eval_scorers import (...)` block inside `validate_dataset_and_gold`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for AC2 — e.g., a unit test (in `tests/unit/test_plan_review_novelty.py` or a sibling) that asserts `verify_findings` / the severity sub-call callable does NOT accept a `prior_findings` parameter (inspect signature) or, if it does accept it for routing, assert it is never forwarded to the LLM prompt constructor.
findings.finding: AC2 ('The severity/validity sub-call receives NO prior findings; Pass-1 receives none') claims independence 'by construction' but specifies no concrete proving command — no unit test, no assertion, no CI check — that would produce evidence this invariant holds after implementation.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC line 2: 'The severity/validity sub-call receives NO prior findings; Pass-1 receives none'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a test that calls `inspect.signature(verify_findings)` and asserts `prior_findings` is not a parameter, OR that the prompt-builder for the severity call is invoked with no prior-findings argument — whichever matches the implementation boundary.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit thin vertical slice: sequence AC delivery so the core runtime path (score_novelty + novelty_model contract + fallback + FakeRunner unit test) is verified green before the eval scaffolding (discriminates_novelty scorer, dedicated eval spec, NOVELTY_EXPECTS balance check) is built — or split into two child tickets on that boundary.
findings.finding: The plan touches ≥7 files across ≥4 architectural layers (LLM contract, scoring arithmetic, eval/scorer registry, test), introduces 6+ new symbols/interfaces, and carries 9 acceptance criteria — well above the G5 decompose thresholds — yet it presents no thin vertical slice or evidence-gated sequencing. The riskiest piece (LLM sub-call correctness + structural independence) is not explicitly de-risked before the eval scaffolding is built.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all 9 items) / Data flow section
findings.suggested_fix: Phase 1 (runtime core): AC lines 1–5 (score_novelty, independence guarantee, NOVELTY_SUBANSWERS, fallback + test, novelty_model contract). Phase 2 (eval gate): AC lines 6–9 (discriminates_novelty scorer, dedicated spec, NOVELTY_EXPECTS, balance check). cc5b remains gated on Phase 2.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Justify why an LLM generative sub-call is preferred over embedding-based semantic similarity (e.g., cosine distance over text-embedding-3-small) for carryover detection, or add a brief note that embedding approaches were considered and rejected (e.g., due to dependency on a vector store, cold-start latency, or lack of location/fix decomposition).
findings.finding: The plan commits to an LLM sub-call for carryover detection without considering embedding-based semantic similarity — a well-established, cheaper alternative for the exact problem of detecting 'same finding, rephrased' across plan revisions.
findings.location: ## Why an LLM sub-call and not the deterministic `sidecar.norm_id` fingerprint
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to the 'Why an LLM sub-call' section: 'Embedding similarity was considered but requires a vector embedding call and produces a scalar distance without the structured (defect/location/fix) decomposition needed to set matched_prior_id and explain the match; the structured sub-call is cheaper to validate and produces an auditable, criterion-aligned rationale.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a rationale for the 3-sub-question decomposition (vs a single holistic 'same defect?' question), addressing in particular how Q2 (location) and Q3 (fix) should behave when the defect is the same but the plan context has changed.
findings.finding: The plan does not justify why the novelty sub-call uses exactly 3 factored sub-questions (restates_prior_defect / cites_prior_location / matches_prior_fix) rather than a single holistic 'is this the same finding?' question — a novel scoring decomposition with no stated rationale.
findings.location: ## Novelty sub-answer set + scoring arithmetic (canonical) — Q1/Q2/Q3 definition

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
